reason: Both answers are substantively identical: correct LTV ($3,200), LTV:CAC (4:1), CAC payback (~8.3 months), and correctly identify 3% monthly (~31% annual) churn as the weak spot needing investor scrutiny. Both conclude "fundable but churn is the concern" with actionable next steps (NRR, cohort curves, churn reduction). Differences are trivial stylistic choices (A adds an average lifetime figure of ~33 months; B frames it as seed/early-stage and suggests cohort retention curves). Neither has an edge in correctness or usefulness.
